nuggets.headline: Even for a band as celebrated as Radiohead, their 'In Rainbows' album nearly didn't happen, pushing them to the brink.
nuggets.text: The creation of 'In Rainbows' was reportedly Radiohead's most challenging album to date, with the band describing the writing process as difficult and even considering an end to their musical journey. After completing their contract with EMI and initially working without longtime producer Nigel Godrich, the members felt 'lost' and faced intense soul-searching about their future as a band.

source.title: medium.com
source.quoteSnippet: After the release of their 2004 album Hail To The Thief, the members of Radiohead were lost. The writing process for their upcoming 7th album was described by many members of the band as being their most difficult, stretching from 2005 all the way to its release in 2007.

nuggets.headline: The hypnotic flow of 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' owes a subtle nod to a pioneering minimalist composer.
nuggets.text: The song's distinctive, shimmering quality is largely due to a 'phasing' technique inspired by American minimalist composer Steve Reich. Rather than relying on a changing drum beat to build energy, the momentum in 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' comes from the intricate layering of multiple arpeggiated guitar parts, creating a captivating, almost polyrhythmic texture that eventually fades to a sudden, dramatic halt, like water vanishing.

source.title: medium.com
source.quoteSnippet: 'Weird Fishes / Arpeggi' features a phasing technique influenced by the American composer Steve Reich.

nuggets.headline: If 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' drew you into its ethereal depths, you absolutely need to explore the hushed, expansive world of Low Roar.
nuggets.text: Much like Radiohead's ability to craft intricate, emotional soundscapes, the Icelandic project Low Roar, led by the late Ryan Karazija, excels in building atmospheric, often melancholic tracks with delicate instrumentation and haunting vocals. Their songs, such as 'I'll Keep Coming' or 'Slow Down,' offer a similar sense of spaciousness and introspective beauty that's perfect for a deep, contemplative listen.

source.title: medium.com
source.quoteSnippet: Low Roar has a brunch of Songs similar to the vibe you're lookong for!
